description: A designed vertical 'self-deportation' promotional graphic. The full-bleed background is a warm, soft-focus photograph set in what appears to be an airport terminal: two people embrace in an emotional hug/farewell. The person facing the camera is a woman wearing eyeglasses and a denim jacket, hugging a man in a white shirt; a handbag hangs from a shoulder and the top of rolling luggage is visible in the foreground, reinforcing the travel/departure theme. Overlaid white text reads: 'If you self-deport now, you MAY be able to return as an immigrant or visitor someday —' followed by a large headline with the key word in green: 'the LEGAL way'. A solid dark-blue band across the bottom holds the DHS seal, the white 'ICE' wordmark, and 'Learn more at: ICE.gov/self-deportation'. Style is an emotive, hopeful lifestyle-photo ad emphasizing reunion and legality (green = go/positive). Messaging technique: positive-incentive persuasion encouraging unauthorized immigrants to voluntarily depart by dangling a possible future legal return, softening a deportation message with warmth and family imagery. Target audience: undocumented immigrants (encouraging voluntary self-removal).
notable_text: If you self-deport now, you MAY be able to return as an immigrant or visitor someday — the LEGAL way; Learn more at: ICE.gov/self-deportation
